data.title: Virtuix Expands Military Training Reach With Marine Corps Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Virtuix Holdings Inc. Class A ( (VTIX) ) has issued an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Virtuix announced that the U.S. Marine Corps Training and Education Command, via partner KBR, purchased an Omni One omni-directional treadmill system for experimentation in warfighter training and mission planning. The unit is slated for delivery to TECOM’s Integration Lab in Quantico, Virginia, in April 2026, where it will be evaluated as part of broader efforts to enhance immersive military training capabilities.

The deal extends Virtuix’s defense footprint, which already includes deployments at the U.S. Military Academy at West Point, the U.S. Air Force Academy, and Yokota Air Base, underscoring growing interest in full-body VR for operational planning and combat readiness. The company views the Marine Corps deployment as validation of its Virtual Terrain Walk technology and a potential catalyst for larger defense contracts, signaling strengthening traction across multiple branches of the U.S. military and a deeper role in next-generation simulation and training.

More about Virtuix Holdings Inc. Class A

Virtuix Inc., listed on Nasdaq as VTIX, is a manufacturer of full-body virtual reality systems serving consumer, enterprise, and defense markets. Its flagship Omni-branded omni-directional treadmills let users walk and run in 360 degrees inside games and other VR applications, while its Virtual Terrain Walk platform uses XR and AI to create immersive, geo-specific training environments for clients worldwide.

Founded on immersive simulation technologies, Virtuix has increasingly targeted defense and military training use cases, positioning its systems as tools for realistic mission planning and combat readiness. By integrating AI-driven 3D reconstruction with VR movement hardware, the company aims to deepen its foothold across multiple U.S. military branches and expand its presence in the broader defense technology ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 422,765

Current Market Cap: $29.34M

data.title: CDW Announces Planned Board Transition as Mehrotra Departs
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from CDW ( (CDW) ) is now available.

On March 24, 2026, CDW Corporation announced that board member Sanjay Mehrotra informed the company he will not stand for reelection at the 2026 Annual Meeting of Stockholders and will serve through the remainder of his current term. The company specified that Mehrotra’s decision is not related to any disagreement over CDW’s operations, policies, or practices, suggesting an orderly board transition with no apparent governance dispute or strategic rift.

This planned departure may prompt CDW to reassess its board composition and refresh its mix of expertise, particularly given Mehrotra’s industry experience. Investors and other stakeholders are likely to view the move as routine succession planning, as the company has emphasized continuity and the absence of conflict behind the decision.

The most recent analyst rating on (CDW) stock is a Buy with a $139.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CDW stock, see the CDW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CDW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CDW is a Neutral.

CDW scores as a steady, cash-generative business with reasonable valuation, supported by earnings-call guidance for modest EPS growth and continued capital returns. The score is held back by leverage and margin/expense pressures, plus mixed technicals with weaker longer-term trend signals and near-term demand unevenness risks.

To see Spark’s full report on CDW stock, click here.

More about CDW

CDW Corporation is a technology solutions provider serving business, government, education, and healthcare customers with a broad portfolio of IT products and services. The company focuses on delivering hardware, software, and integrated IT solutions designed to support digital transformation and infrastructure needs across diverse end markets.

data.title: EDA Group flags 2025 loss on overseas expansion and rising costs
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

EDA Group Holdings Limited ( (HK:2505) ) has provided an announcement.

EDA Group Holdings Limited has warned that it expects to swing from a net profit of about RMB47.1 million in 2024 to a net loss of roughly RMB12 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, with adjusted results shifting from a profit of RMB113.9 million to an adjusted net loss of around RMB8.5 million. The downturn is mainly attributed to upfront costs and amortisation from strategic expansion into new overseas warehouses, pressure on order unit prices from tariff policy changes and heightened competition, higher overseas logistics and labour expenses, and a larger provision for expected credit losses as receivables age, signalling near-term margin pressure even as management frames the warehouse investments as critical to sustaining long-term growth.

The board underscored that the adjusted net loss measure excludes listing and share-based payment expenses, which it views as non-operational or one-off items, to provide a clearer view of underlying performance. Final audited results for 2025 have yet to be completed and may differ from these preliminary figures, leaving investors to weigh short-term earnings weakness against the potential strategic benefits of the company’s expanded international logistics infrastructure.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2505) stock is a Hold with a HK$1.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on EDA Group Holdings Limited stock, see the HK:2505 Stock Forecast page.

More about EDA Group Holdings Limited

EDA Group Holdings Limited operates in the cross-border logistics and warehousing sector, focusing on overseas warehouse leasing and related logistics services for international trade customers. The group has been expanding its footprint by adding new leased overseas warehouses since the second half of 2024 to support long-term growth in global markets.

Average Trading Volume: 157,964

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$263.7M

See more data about 2505 stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: China Coal Energy Profit Falls 20% in 2025 as Revenue Slumps
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Coal Energy Co ( (HK:1898) ) just unveiled an announcement.

China Coal Energy Co reported a sharp decline in 2025 financial performance, with revenue falling 21.8% year on year to RMB148.06 billion as weaker coal prices and lower trading volumes weighed on the top line. Profit attributable to equity holders dropped 20% to RMB14.50 billion, while basic earnings per share slid to RMB1.09 and EBITDA contracted 18.2% to RMB33.17 billion.

Despite the earnings pressure, the board recommended a final dividend of RMB0.217 per share for 2025, signaling an ongoing commitment to shareholder returns even in a softer profit environment. Lower costs across materials, sales taxes and other operating items partially offset the revenue decline, but reduced operating profit and higher effective tax still compressed net results, underscoring the impact of a less favorable market backdrop on the coal producer’s profitability.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1898) stock is a Buy with a HK$11.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Coal Energy Co stock, see the HK:1898 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Coal Energy Co

China Coal Energy Co is a major Chinese coal producer operating as a joint stock company in the People’s Republic of China, with its shares listed in Hong Kong. The group is engaged in coal mining, coal trading and related energy businesses, generating revenue primarily from the production and sale of coal and coal-related products.

Average Trading Volume: 22,794,686

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$243.6B

Shandong Weigao Group Medical Polymer Co ( (HK:1066) ) has provided an announcement.

Shandong Weigao Group Medical Polymer Company Limited, a Hong Kong-listed Chinese medical device maker specializing in polymer-based products, oversees a broad portfolio that includes its orthopaedic subsidiary, Shandong Weigao Orthopaedic Device Company Limited. data.title: Qyuns Therapeutics Tightens Audit Committee Mandate to Bolster Governance
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Qyuns Therapeutics Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2509) ) has shared an update.

Qyuns Therapeutics has formalized detailed terms of reference for its board Audit Committee to enhance decision-making and oversight of financial reporting and internal control systems. The committee is mandated to review the company’s financial condition and data, assess the authenticity and accuracy of financial information, supervise communications with external auditors, and guide internal audit work to strengthen internal controls and risk management.

The document also sets structural requirements for the Audit Committee, which must comprise at least three non-executive directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors and at least one member holding appropriate accounting or financial management qualifications in line with Hong Kong Listing Rules. The company commits to providing necessary resources and cooperation from management to ensure the committee can effectively execute its responsibilities, signaling a reinforced governance framework for investors and other stakeholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2509) stock is a Hold with a HK$22.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Qyuns Therapeutics Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:2509 Stock Forecast page.

More about Qyuns Therapeutics Co., Ltd. Class H

Qyuns Therapeutics Co., Ltd. is a pharmaceutical company operating in the therapeutics sector and listed in Hong Kong. The company is governed under the Company Law of the People’s Republic of China and the Hong Kong Listing Rules, and it emphasizes robust financial reporting, internal controls, and audit oversight as part of its corporate governance framework.

data.title: KKCG Maritime Raises Ferretti Tender Offer Price and Issues Clarification
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ferretti S.p.A. ( (IT:YACHT) ) just unveiled an announcement.

KKCG Maritime, through Azúr A.S., has raised its voluntary conditional partial public tender offer for up to 52,132,861 Ferretti shares, equivalent to 15.4% of the yacht maker’s share capital, increasing the offer price from €3.50 to €3.90 per share on a cum-dividend basis. The revised price represents notable premiums over recent trading levels in both Milan and Hong Kong and is designed to maximise shareholder participation, support KKCG Maritime’s aim of strengthening its board representation and strategic influence at Ferretti, and fix the final consideration level, as no further price increases will be allowed under takeover rules.

KKCG Maritime also issued a clarification correcting an error in the English offer document supplement regarding the description of price differentials, ensuring that investors have accurate information on the premium and discount calculations associated with the revised consideration. This clarification, together with the higher offer price, seeks to remove ambiguities for shareholders evaluating the tender and may affect market perceptions of Ferretti’s valuation and control dynamics.

More about Ferretti S.p.A.

Ferretti S.p.A. is a yacht manufacturer listed in Hong Kong and on Euronext Milan, operating in the luxury maritime sector under the ticker EXM: YACHT. The company focuses on high-end leisure vessels, serving affluent clients and investors attracted to the premium boating and marine lifestyle market.

data.title: ENM Holdings swings back to profit on stronger operating gains
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

ENM Holdings ( (HK:0128) ) has issued an announcement.

ENM Holdings reported a sharp turnaround in its 2025 results, with group profit attributable to shareholders rising to HK$24.3 million from a loss of HK$7.9 million a year earlier, driven by higher other operating gains and a return to profitability in discontinued operations despite fair value losses on investment properties. Earnings per share improved to 1.47 cents from a loss of 0.48 cents, signaling a marked recovery in overall performance and offering some reassurance to investors about the stability of its continuing operations and the successful wind-down or restructuring of discontinued segments.

From its core continuing operations, the company’s revenue edged up to HK$19.9 million and profit surged to HK$22.0 million from HK$2.6 million, reflecting stronger other income and operating gains that offset steady administrative and depreciation costs. The improved profitability, combined with the absence of income tax expense and minimal finance costs, underscores a lean balance sheet and suggests that future results may remain sensitive to movements in investment property valuations and one-off gains rather than purely recurring operating income.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0128) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.39 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on ENM Holdings stock, see the HK:0128 Stock Forecast page.

More about ENM Holdings

ENM Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated investment holding company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The group’s continuing operations generate modest revenue streams and maintain investment properties, focusing on managing assets and related income rather than large-scale industrial or consumer operations. Its shareholder base is primarily concerned with capital preservation and incremental earnings growth in a relatively small-scale business profile.

Average Trading Volume: 1,568,700

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$685M

data.title: Huili Resources Profit Slumps as Revenue Halves and One-Off Gains Fade
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Huili Resources (Group) Limited ( (HK:1303) ) has shared an announcement.

Huili Resources (Group) Limited reported a sharp decline in revenue to RMB2.26 billion for 2025 from RMB4.03 billion a year earlier, with gross profit falling to RMB132.5 million. The group’s profit from continuing operations shrank significantly, as higher administrative costs, increased expected credit losses on financial assets and net other losses drove operating profit down to RMB17.9 million from RMB173.8 million.

Net profit attributable to equity owners dropped to RMB11.6 million in 2025 from RMB160.3 million in 2024, reflecting the absence of contributions from discontinued operations and prior-year one-off gains. While net finance income turned positive, it was not enough to offset weaker operating performance, highlighting pressure on margins and earnings that may affect shareholder returns and signal a more challenging operating environment for the company.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1303) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.23 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Huili Resources (Group) Limited stock, see the HK:1303 Stock Forecast page.

More about Huili Resources (Group) Limited

Huili Resources (Group) Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the resources sector. The group generates revenue primarily from the sale of its resource-related products, positioning itself within the broader commodities and mining supply chain in mainland China and surrounding markets.

data.title: Technovator Revamps Board Leadership With New Chairman and Non-Executive Director
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Technovator International ( (HK:1206) ) has issued an update.

Technovator International announced boardroom changes effective 27 March 2026, with chairman and non-executive director Li Chengfu and non-executive director Zhang Yanhua resigning from the board and its committees due to work arrangement adjustments, with both stating they had no disagreement with the board. The company has appointed Han Tao as non-executive director, board chairman, committee member and authorised representative, and Gao Peifeng as non-executive director and risk committee member, signaling a refresh of its governance structure and potential strategic influence from Han’s management experience at Tongfang-linked entities and state-backed investment firms.

Han Tao, a senior engineer with degrees in nuclear engineering from Tsinghua University and more than a decade of corporate management and project administration experience, has signed a one-year, automatically renewable service contract with no initial director fee, subject to review and shareholder-authorised board adjustments. His lack of other listed directorships, roles within the group, or ties to major shareholders underscores an independent profile within the non-executive ranks, while the appointments collectively aim to maintain regulatory compliance and continuity in Technovator’s leadership amid the transitions.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1206) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Technovator International stock, see the HK:1206 Stock Forecast page.

More about Technovator International

Technovator International Limited is a Singapore-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates through a group structure, though the announcement focuses primarily on its board and governance. The group is overseen by a board of directors and various committees, including nomination, remuneration and risk management, reflecting a standard listed-company governance framework and regulatory compliance focus.

Average Trading Volume: 370,509

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$226.8M
